title: MEDIUM: Push notification client follows redirects, enabling SSRF bypass
description:
The PUSH_CLIENT in push.rs (line 325-330) is built without .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none()), unlike all other HTTP clients in the codebase (federation, delivery, cards).

This means a push endpoint URL like https://legit-push-service.example/push could return a 302 redirect to http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ and the client would follow it, bypassing the validate_outbound_url() check at line 297 which only validates the initial URL.

The SSRF check on the endpoint URL happens before the request, but if the push service responds with a redirect to an internal IP, the redirect will be followed. reqwest's default is to follow up to 10 redirects.

Fix: add .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none()) to the PUSH_CLIENT builder:
```rust
static PUSH_CLIENT: LazyLock<reqwest::Client> = LazyLock::new(|| {
    reqwest::Client::builder()
        .timeout(Duration::from_secs(30))
        .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none())
        .build()
        .expect("failed to build push HTTP client")
});
```

File: src/push.rs lines 325-330
close_reason: Fixed: PUSH_CLIENT now uses redirect Policy::none()
